criteria.name: Valuation Method Coverage
criteria.1: Only one valuation method presented, no comparison or cross-check, incomplete analysis
criteria.3: Two methods presented (e.g., DCF and one relative), basic comparison made, key assumptions noted for each
criteria.5: Three or more methods presented (DCF, peer comparison, regression), all in reconciliation table with confidence weights, assumptions, and per-share values clearly documented

criteria.name: Divergence Analysis
criteria.1: No analysis of why different methods produce different values, differences ignored or dismissed
criteria.3: Divergence noted and attributed to general causes (different assumptions), some investigation of which specific inputs drive the spread
criteria.5: Systematic diagnosis of divergence sources (growth, risk, peer selection, capital structure), each gap traced to specific assumptions, resolution documented with rationale for weighting

criteria.name: Implied Assumption Plausibility
criteria.1: No reverse-engineering of market assumptions, or implied values stated without any plausibility check
criteria.3: Implied growth rate or ROIC calculated, compared to at least one benchmark (historical, industry, or base rate)
criteria.5: Both implied growth and implied ROIC calculated, compared to company history, industry median, and base rates for sustainability, interpretation clearly links to narrative disagreement

criteria.name: Catalyst Specificity
criteria.1: No catalysts identified, or catalysts are vague (the market will recognize value eventually)
criteria.3: At least one specific catalyst with approximate timing, some discussion of expected impact
criteria.5: Two or more catalysts with specific events, estimated timing (quarter or date), quantified impact ($/share or %), probability assessment, and independence check

criteria.name: Margin of Safety Calibration
criteria.1: Margin of safety not computed, or computed but not calibrated to company risk profile
criteria.3: Margin of safety computed, some acknowledgment that required margin varies by company type
criteria.5: Margin of safety computed and explicitly calibrated to uncertainty dimensions (cash flow predictability, forecast horizon, information quality), actual margin compared to required margin, recommendation adjusted if insufficient

criteria.name: Recommendation Clarity
criteria.1: No clear recommendation, or recommendation without price target, time horizon, or conviction level
criteria.3: Buy/sell/hold stated with price target and time horizon, basic rationale provided
criteria.5: Buy/sell/hold with specific price target range, time horizon, conviction level, clear rationale linking narrative to valuation to recommendation, upgrade/downgrade triggers stated

criteria.name: Thesis Completeness
criteria.1: No investment thesis, or thesis is a single unsupported sentence
criteria.3: Thesis covers business narrative and valuation summary, links story to numbers, includes basic recommendation
criteria.5: Complete one-page thesis linking narrative, numbers, market view, value gap, catalysts, risks, and actionable recommendation with exit triggers

criteria.name: Risk Factor Coverage
criteria.1: No risk factors identified, or generic risks (the stock could go down) with no specificity
criteria.3: Two or more specific risk factors stated, some assessment of probability or impact
criteria.5: Three or more specific, company-relevant risk factors with probability and impact assessment, explicit statement of what would invalidate the thesis, stop-loss or exit trigger defined

key_requirements: Multiple valuation methods confirm undervaluation (DCF and at least one relative method)
Implied growth rate materially below narrative growth, with plausibility check against base rates
At least one specific catalyst with timing within 12 months and quantified impact
Margin of safety exceeds minimum for company type (e.g., 20%+ for moderate-uncertainty company)
Risk factors explicitly state what could make the buy thesis wrong
common_pitfalls: Value trap: stock is cheap for a reason (declining business, structural headwinds) that the analysis ignores
Catalyst-free thesis: correct valuation but no mechanism for market to reprice
Ignoring recent price decline cause: stock dropped 30% before analysis -- check if new information invalidates prior assumptions
Insufficient margin for company risk: 15% margin on a volatile, unpredictable business

key_requirements: DCF value materially below market price, supported by at least one relative valuation method
Implied growth rate or ROIC exceeds plausible ranges (checked against base rates and industry data)
Risk factors for the current price level identified (what could cause the stock to decline)
Recommendation acknowledges momentum risk (expensive stocks can stay expensive or get more expensive)
common_pitfalls: Underestimating optionality in high-growth companies (real options not captured in DCF)
Anchoring on historical multiples when business model has structurally changed
Ignoring short-squeeze or momentum risk when recommending sell
Failing to specify a time horizon for the sell thesis

key_requirements: Multiple methods produce values within 10-15% of market price (convergence documented)
Implied assumptions align with narrative (no significant disagreement with market)
No compelling catalysts for repricing in either direction
Recommendation notes conditions that would upgrade to Buy or downgrade to Sell
common_pitfalls: Lazy Hold: defaulting to Hold without doing the full reconciliation work
Missing asymmetry: stock may be fairly valued on average but have skewed upside/downside
Not specifying what would change the recommendation to Buy or Sell

common_failure_modes.symptom: Only DCF presented, or only one multiple cited, with no cross-validation
common_failure_modes.detection: Reconciliation table has only one row, or relative valuation methods are absent
common_failure_modes.fix: Include at least two methods (DCF + one relative), preferably three, and document why each is relevant

common_failure_modes.symptom: All methods produce nearly identical values (suspiciously tight cluster), or the method supporting the desired conclusion gets 80%+ weight
common_failure_modes.detection: Value spread < 5% across methods, or single method dominates without justification
common_failure_modes.fix: Review whether assumptions were unconsciously aligned across methods; justify confidence weights based on method appropriateness, not on which answer you prefer

common_failure_modes.symptom: Analysis jumps from DCF value > price to Buy without examining what the market is pricing in
common_failure_modes.detection: No implied growth rate or implied ROIC calculation, no comparison of market view vs analyst view
common_failure_modes.fix: Back-solve for the growth rate and ROIC that make DCF = price; compare to your narrative and check plausibility

common_failure_modes.symptom: Buy recommendation with no mechanism for the gap to close, or catalysts like 'market will recognize value'
common_failure_modes.detection: No catalyst section, or catalysts lack timing, impact quantification, or probability
common_failure_modes.fix: Identify at least one specific event with estimated timing (quarter or date), expected impact ($/share), and probability

common_failure_modes.symptom: Same margin of safety threshold (e.g., 20%) applied to all companies regardless of risk profile
common_failure_modes.detection: No discussion of why the margin is sufficient for this particular company's uncertainty level
common_failure_modes.fix: Assess cash flow predictability, forecast horizon sensitivity, and information quality; adjust required margin accordingly

common_failure_modes.symptom: Buy recommendation with no downside discussion, or generic risks that apply to any stock
common_failure_modes.detection: Risk section absent, or risks like 'macroeconomic downturn' without company-specific connection
common_failure_modes.fix: Identify at least two company-specific risks, state probability and impact, define what would cause recommendation reversal

common_failure_modes.symptom: Buy or Sell recommendation without specifying when the thesis should play out
common_failure_modes.detection: Recommendation section lacks time horizon or says 'long term' without definition
common_failure_modes.fix: Specify 6-month, 12-month, or 24-month horizon aligned with catalyst timing; state reassessment triggers

common_failure_modes.symptom: Company deemed cheap relative to peers, but entire sector may be overvalued (or undervalued)
common_failure_modes.detection: Only peer-relative analysis performed; no check against broader market or fundamental anchors
common_failure_modes.fix: Note that relative valuation says 'cheap vs peers' not 'undervalued in absolute terms'; compare sector multiple to historical range or to DCF-implied multiple
